title: jatropha varians
intro: Jatropha varians, also known as the stinking goosefoot or bushy jatropha, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin infections, and other health issues. The leaves and seeds of Jatropha varians have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their health benefits such as antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of Jatropha varians include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
